rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents With Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 12, 2025, line 8, 2 - DEFENDANT PETER SUTER'S Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents With Proof Of Service Defendant Peter Suter's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in his Requests for Admission, Set One, to plaintiff Eric McFadden is granted. McFadden failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set One, is deemed admitted by McFadden. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Suter does not seek sanctions. However, CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $50 payable by McFadden to Suter. McFadden shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiff Eric Mcfaddens Responses Without Objections To Form And Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, Set One With Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 12, 2025, line 8, 1 - DEFENDANT PETER SUTER'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Eric Mcfaddens Responses Without Objections To Form And Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, Set One With Proof Of Service Defendant Peter Suter's motion to compel Plaintiff Eric McFadden to respond to his Form and Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, is granted. McFadden failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2030.290(a), 2031.300(a).) McFadden is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by responsive documents within 10 court days of entry of this order. Suter does not seek sanctions. The court awards none. The court notes intervenor Cody Molica's notice of his incarceration filed on April 30, 2025. Because this motion seeks no relief against Molica, no accommodation is provided. rulings.Case Title: EVAN REEVES VS. GOLDEN STATE WARRIORS, LLC, A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Withdraw Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 12, 2025, line 3, PLAINTIFF EVAN REEVES' Motion To Withdraw Admissions The unopposed motion to withdraw admissions to defendant's RFA Nos. 1-15 is granted. The court finds that the admissions were the result of prior counsel's mistake or neglect and that, in light of defendant's nonopposition, the party who obtained the admission will not be substantially prejudiced in maintaining that party's action or defense on the merits. (See CCP 2033.300(b).) The court further rescinds the sanctions order, again in light of defendant's nonopposition. Plaintiff shall serve verified responses without objections to RFA Nos. 1-15 by June 13, 2025. rulings.Case Title: ARYAN MOTEDAYENI VS. FAIRMONT SAN FRANCISCO, UNKOWN ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT MOHAMMED AZAZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 12, 2025, line 5, DEFENDANT MOHAMMED AZAZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2) The court denies the request for a continuance so that plaintiff may conduct jurisdictional discovery. Generally a continuance for jurisdictional discovery may be had where a plaintiff demonstrates "that discovery is likely to lead to the production of evidence of facts establishing jurisdiction." (In re Automobile Antitrust Cases I & II (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 100, 127.) But service of process itself is what confers power on the court to compel defendant to respond to discovery. Because service was defective here, the court lacks that power. Moreover, plaintiff's request for jurisdictional discovery does not satisfy the standard of showing that discovery is likely to lead to the production of evidence establishing that substitute service was properly made, which is what is necessary here to establish jurisdiction. Even if plaintiff could establish through discovery that defendant resides at or works at the Commack address, contrary to the Azaz declaration, that evidence would not cure plaintiff's process server's failure to provide an effective declaration of diligence. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (end of tentative ruling part 2 of 2) = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ARYAN MOTEDAYENI VS. FAIRMONT SAN FRANCISCO, UNKOWN ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 12, 2025, line 5, DEFENDANT MOHAMMED AZAZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS (tentative ruling part 1 of 2) Defendant Azaz's motion to quash is granted. Plaintiff has the evidentiary burden to demonstrate proper service to warrant this court's exercise of jurisdiction. (Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1440 ["[T]he burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service"].) "[I]n California, the original service of process, which confers jurisdiction, must conform to statutory requirements or all that follows is void." (Ruttenberg v. Ruttenberg (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 801, 809 [internal quotation marks and citation omitted].) Plaintiff relies on Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, for the argument that the service statutes should be liberally construed if the defendant has received actual notice. But as Summers makes clear, actual notice alone is not enough; a service statute may be liberally construed but it may not be set aside, regardless of whether there is actual notice. (Id at p. 415 ["no California appellate court has gone so far as to uphold a service of process solely on the ground the defendant received actual notice when there has been a complete failure to comply with the statutory requirements for service."].) The court grants plaintiff's unopposed request for judicial notice of this court's records per Evidence Code section 452(d). Plaintiff contends that plaintiff substitute-served defendant at 6277 Jericho Turnpike, Commack, NY. (CCP 415.20(b).) The claimed service was defective. The only competent evidence demonstrates that the address is not defendant's dwelling house, usual place of abode, usual place of business, or usual mailing address. (Azaz Decl., pars. 4-6.) Defendant has personal knowledge regarding his domicile and place of work. At most, plaintiff has shown that the property may be affiliated with defendant's family (or defendant's father who shares the same name), but there is no competent evidence to support a finding of proper substitute service and defendant's evidentiary objections have merit. In addition, plaintiff's declaration of diligence is lacking since plaintiff's process server only went to the Commack address once before leaving the papers with Lynn "Doe." (Defendant's RJN, Ex. C.) In sum, plaintiff has not met plaintiff's burden of proof. (end of tentative ruling part 1, see part 2) = (301/CVA)
